**Mushi Production** (虫プロダクション, *Mushi Purodakushon*; "Bug Production") or **Mushi Pro**[[a]](#cite_note-1) for short, is a Japanese animation studio headquartered in Fujimidai, [Nerima, Tokyo](/wiki/Nerima,_Tokyo "Nerima, Tokyo"), Japan.[[1]](#cite_note-2) It previously had a headquarters elsewhere in Nerima.

The studio was headed by manga artist [Osamu Tezuka](/wiki/Osamu_Tezuka "Osamu Tezuka").[[2]](#cite_note-Morisawa-3) Tezuka started it as a rivalry with [Toei Animation](/wiki/Toei_Animation "Toei Animation"), his former employer, after Tezuka's contract with Toei expired in 1961.[[3]](#cite_note-4) The studio pioneered TV animation in Japan, and was responsible for many successful [anime](/wiki/Anime "Anime") television series, such as *[Astro Boy](/wiki/Astro_Boy "Astro Boy")*, *[Gokū no Daibōken](/wiki/Gok%C5%AB_no_Daib%C5%8Dken "Gokū no Daibōken")*, *[Princess Knight](/wiki/Ribon_no_Kishi "Ribon no Kishi")*, *[Kimba the White Lion](/wiki/Kimba_the_White_Lion "Kimba the White Lion")*, *[Dororo](/wiki/Dororo "Dororo")* and *[Ashita no Joe](/wiki/Tomorrow%27s_Joe "Tomorrow's Joe")*, as well as more adult-oriented feature films such as *[A Thousand and One Nights](/wiki/A_Thousand_and_One_Nights_(1969_film) "A Thousand and One Nights (1969 film)")*, *[Cleopatra](/wiki/Cleopatra_(1970_film) "Cleopatra (1970 film)")* (the first Japanese [X-rated](/wiki/X_rating "X rating") animated film) and *[Belladonna of Sadness](/wiki/Belladonna_of_Sadness "Belladonna of Sadness")*.

In addition to doing their anime productions, Mushi was best known for its overseas work on five traditionally animated TV projects from [Arthur Rankin, Jr.](/wiki/Arthur_Rankin,_Jr. "Arthur Rankin, Jr.") and [Jules Bass](/wiki/Jules_Bass "Jules Bass")' Videocraft International (now [Rankin/Bass Productions](/wiki/Rankin/Bass_Productions "Rankin/Bass Productions")) in New York, New York, including the Christmas special *[Frosty the Snowman](/wiki/Frosty_the_Snowman_(TV_special) "Frosty the Snowman (TV special)")*, with the production artwork being done by [Paul Coker, Jr.](/wiki/Paul_Coker,_Jr. "Paul Coker, Jr."), along with the animation supervision by Yusaku "Steve" Nakagawa.

Morisawa argues that Tezuka "proposed an unrealistically suppressed production budget... in an attempt to outbid his competitors", a budget that contributed to the Studio's (and industry at the time) low profitability.[[2]](#cite_note-Morisawa-3) Mushi, plagued by financial difficulties, declared [bankruptcy](/wiki/Bankruptcy "Bankruptcy") in 1973 and its assets were divided.[[2]](#cite_note-Morisawa-3) Tezuka had already left the company by then, having stepped down as acting director in 1968 and formed a new animation studio, [Tezuka Productions](/wiki/Tezuka_Productions "Tezuka Productions") (which made such works as *[Marvelous Melmo](/wiki/Marvelous_Melmo "Marvelous Melmo")* and *[Unico](/wiki/Unico "Unico")*). The company was later reestablished on November 26, 1977, and has continued to operate as "legacy company".

The name "Mushi" means "insect" in Japanese, which is significant because Tezuka had a deep affection for insects, particularly since he was a fan of [entomology](/wiki/Entomology "Entomology") from a young age. This passion is reflected in his pen name, "Osamu Tezuka," where "Tezuka" is derived from the word for "[firefly](/wiki/Firefly "Firefly")" in Japanese.

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mushi_Production&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Tama Production](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E3%82%BF%E3%83%9E%E3%83%BB%E3%83%97%E3%83%AD%E3%83%80%E3%82%AF%E3%82%B7%E3%83%A7%E3%83%B3 "jp:タマ・プロダクション"), an animation studio founded in 1965 by former Mushi Production animator Eiji Tanaka, but gone bankrupt in 2011
* [Tezuka Productions](/wiki/Tezuka_Productions "Tezuka Productions"), an animation studio founded in 1968 as a spun-off division by Tezuka
* [Group TAC](/wiki/Group_TAC "Group TAC"), an animation studio founded by former Mushi Productions employees, including sound effects director Atsumi Tashiro, and animators Susumu Akitagawa and [Gisaburo Sugii](/wiki/Gisaburo_Sugii "Gisaburo Sugii"), went bankrupt in 2010
* [Madhouse](/wiki/Madhouse_(company) "Madhouse (company)"), an animation studio founded by former Mushi Production animators, including [Masao Maruyama](/wiki/Masao_Maruyama_(film_producer) "Masao Maruyama (film producer)"), [Osamu Dezaki](/wiki/Osamu_Dezaki "Osamu Dezaki"), [Rintaro](/wiki/Rintaro "Rintaro") and [Yoshiaki Kawajiri](/wiki/Yoshiaki_Kawajiri "Yoshiaki Kawajiri"), now became a subsidiary of [Nippon Television](/wiki/Nippon_Television "Nippon Television")
* [Sunrise](/wiki/Sunrise_(company) "Sunrise (company)"), an animation studio founded by former Mushi Production animators, now part of [Bandai Namco Holdings](/wiki/Bandai_Namco_Holdings "Bandai Namco Holdings")
* [Studio Pierrot](/wiki/Pierrot_(company) "Pierrot (company)"), an animation studio founded by former Mushi Pro employees, along with the other employees from [Tatsunoko](/wiki/Tatsunoko_Production "Tatsunoko Production")
* [Kyoto Animation](/wiki/Kyoto_Animation "Kyoto Animation"), an animation studio founded in [Kyoto](/wiki/Kyoto "Kyoto") by former Mushi Pro staff, was involved in an [arson attack in 2019](/wiki/Kyoto_Animation_arson_attack "Kyoto Animation arson attack")
* [Shaft](/wiki/Shaft_(company) "Shaft (company)"), a studio formerly concerned with [cel work](/wiki/Cel "Cel") that eventually branched off into original productions, founded in 1975 by Hiroshi Wakao
* [Studio Gallop](/wiki/Studio_Gallop "Studio Gallop"), animated studio founded in 1978 by former Mushi Pro staffs
